statement.markdown: Given a string containing just the characters '(' and ')', find the maximum nesting depth of parentheses. The maximum nesting depth is defined as the maximum number of open parentheses that do not have a corresponding close parenthesis yet. For example, in the string '(())', the maximum nesting depth is 2 because there are two open parentheses that do not have a corresponding close parenthesis until the second close parenthesis. Similarly, in '(())()', the maximum nesting depth is still 2.

constraints.description: The input string containing just the characters '(' and ')'.

hints.text: Initialize a variable to keep track of the maximum nesting depth: max_depth = 0.
Iterate through the string character by character.
For each character, check if it is an opening parenthesis ('('): if so, increment max_depth and return if it reaches the length of the string.
If the character is a closing parenthesis (')', check if there is an unmatched opening parenthesis before it: if so, increment max_depth and return.
If the character is neither an opening nor a closing parenthesis, continue to the next character.

editorial.title: Maximum Nesting Depth of Parentheses
editorial.intuition: The problem requires finding the maximum nesting depth of parentheses in a given string. A key observation is that we only need to keep track of the current depth and the maximum depth encountered so far.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would involve using a stack to push every opening parenthesis and pop for every closing parenthesis, keeping track of the maximum size of the stack. This approach, while correct, is slow because it requires extra space and more operations.
editorial.betterSolution: The optimal approach uses a single pass through the string, keeping track of the current depth and updating the maximum depth as needed.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution iterates through the string once, maintaining a running count of the current depth and updating the maximum depth whenever the current depth exceeds it. This approach achieves a time complexity of O(n) and a space complexity of O(1).
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the optimal solution can be proven by observing that the current depth accurately reflects the nesting depth at each position in the string. The maximum depth is updated whenever a deeper nesting level is encountered, ensuring that the final maximum depth is correct.
editorial.dryRun: Testing the optimal solution with sample inputs, such as strings with varying nesting depths, confirms its correctness and efficiency.
commonMistakes: Using a stack to store every parenthesis, leading to extra space usage
Failing to update the maximum depth when the current depth exceeds it
Not handling the case where the input string contains only one type of parenthesis
Not considering the time and space complexity of the solution
Incorrectly assuming that the input string is empty or contains invalid characters
Not verifying the correctness of the solution with test cases
editorial.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is the length of the string
